Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to contain calendar, facet, session, template, soft-error, and query-cardinality traps with stop evidence; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
